mission.title: Install and Configure Tremor on Kubernetes
mission.description: Tremor is an event- or stream-processing system designed for high-volumetric data, providing low latency and efficient resource usage. Installing Tremor allows you to handle ETL workloads effectively, making it a valuable tool for data processing in production environments.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Tremor Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add tremor https://charts.tremor.rs
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Tremor
steps.description:
Install the Tremor Helm chart in a new namespace called 'tremor'.
```bash
helm install tremor tremor/tremor --namespace tremor --create-namespace --version v0.13.0-rc.33
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Tremor pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace tremor
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Update the Tremor deployment to set resource limits for CPU and memory.
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment/tremor --namespace tremor --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi
```

steps.title: Set up port forwarding for access
steps.description:
Forward a local port to the Tremor service to access it.
```bash
kubectl port-forward service/tremor --namespace tremor 8080:80
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Tremor release from the cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall tremor --namespace tremor
```

uninstall.title: Delete the Tremor namespace
uninstall.description:
Remove the Tremor namespace to clean up all associated resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace tremor
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current configuration and state of the Tremor deployment.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace tremor -o yaml > tremor-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure the Helm repository is up to date with the latest charts.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Tremor installation
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the Tremor release to the latest version.
```bash
helm upgrade tremor tremor/tremor --namespace tremor --version v0.13.0-rc.33
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the Tremor pods to ensure they are running the new version.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace tremor
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If the Tremor pods are stuck in a Pending state, check for resource availability.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace tremor
``` 
Ensure there are enough resources in the cluster or adjust resource requests/limits.

troubleshooting.title: Tremor service not responding
troubleshooting.description:
If the service is not responding, check the logs of the Tremor pods for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace tremor
``` 
Look for any error messages that indicate configuration issues.

troubleshooting.title: Helm upgrade fails
troubleshooting.description:
If the Helm upgrade fails, check the Helm release history for errors.
```bash
helm history tremor --namespace tremor
``` 
You can rollback to a previous version if necessary using:
```bash
helm rollback tremor <revision-number> --namespace tremor
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Tremor will have all pods running in the 'tremor' namespace, and you will be able to access the service via the forwarded port. Resource limits will be configured, ensuring optimal performance.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add tremor https://charts.tremor.rs
helm repo update
helm install tremor tremor/tremor --namespace tremor --create-namespace --version v0.13.0-rc.33

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and Helm installed on your local machine.
